page: Amazon Web Services c4.2xlarge is a compute-optimized fourth-generation instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor running at 2.9 GHz, featuring 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 15.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 1.88 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network performance is anchored by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the average tier for its hardware class. The single-core Geekbench score is 1182.0, while the multi-core score reaches 4493.0. In stress-ng testing, it achieves a single-core CPU score of 1470.98 and a multi-core score of 7763.44. Memory bandwidth tests show 423.32 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 50.6 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Redis SET operations show weaker relative performance. Large-model LLM inference is also a bottleneck, with the 7B model achieving only 11.57 tokens per second for prompt processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-to-memory profile for legacy workloads requiring dedicated Intel Xeon cores without the overhead of modern, more expensive architectures. It is well-suited for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and moderate image processing. It is less optimal for memory-intensive databases or modern large language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c4.2xlarge is a compute-optimized, fourth-generation instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Xen hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 15.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute tasks, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 4493.0 and a static web serving throughput of 6.85 GB/sec. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and Redis operations. The c4.2xlarge is best suited for dedicated compute workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and basic image processing, offering a stable, legacy hardware profile for non-database-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c4.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 15.0 GB RAM, and a 2.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering reliable mid-tier performance for static web serving and compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c4.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 15.0 GB RAM, and a 2.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory for standard web serving and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2666 v3 processor with 8 vCPUs and 15.0 GB of RAM
Dedicated x86_64 CPU cores running at 2.9 GHz under Xen hypervisor
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Average performance in software compilation and static web serving
Weaker performance in Redis operations and large-model LLM inference
Suitable for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and legacy applications
